narrative.analyst_commentary: The business outlook for British American Tobacco remains solid. During an early-December trading update, the company noted that it was reaffirming guidance for 2025. However, it now expects sales to rise at a 2% clip, which is at the top end of its prior forecast of 1%-2% growth. The advance was likely driven by the New Categories division, which includes vapor, tobacco heating, and modern oral goods. That business, which accounted for roughly 14% of total first-half revenues, likely rose at a double-digit rate in the second half of 2025. We think the growth was largely driven by recent rollouts of new products. Demand for modern oral goods remains robust. Sales growth in the U. S. was probably fueled by an improving combustibles performance and strong demand for Velo Plus nicotine patches. We think growth in earnings per ADR in the December half was supported by the higher sales and a good operating performance from the New Categories segment. We look for decent topand bottom-line growth in 2026. The gains will likely continue to be driven by the New Categories segment, thanks in part to management’s ongoing investments in the group. Velo will probably lead the advance, as it is the fastest growing product line in that business. Management also noted that it was seeing encouraging performances for Vuse Ultra in certain markets, including Canada, Germany, and France. However, revenue gains in the smokeless business will probably be partly offset by ongoing declines in global cigarette volumes. Earnings per ADR will likely rise at a low-to mid-single digit rate. The advance ought to be supported by the higher sales, a lower share count, and operational efficiency initiatives. British American Tobacco ADRs are ranked to track the broader market averages over the coming six to 12 months. The primary draw here remains the dividend yield of 5.3%, which ought to pique the interest of income-seeking accounts. The stock also has a Safety rank of 2 (Above Average) and a high score for Price Stability. However, over the 3 to 5 year period, the equity offers unappealing total return potential.
narrative.business: British American Tobacco is one of the world’s leading consumer goods companies. The company engages in the manufacture, distribution, and sale of tobacco products, primarily cigarettes. It also produces vapour and tobacco heating products, as well as oral tobacco and nicotine goods. Acquired the remaining 48% stake of Reynolds American that it didn’t already own in July 2017, which allowed it to become the largest publicly-traded tobacco company in the world. Brands include Pall Mall, Camel, Newport, Lucky Strike, Dunhill and Kent. Has 48,000 employees as of 12/31/24. CEO: Tadeu Marroco. Incorporated: United Kingdom. Address: Globe House, 4 Temple Place, London WC 2 R 2 PG. Telephone: 44-20-7845-1000. Internet: www.bat.com.
